indicator_text: In addition to legislative lobbyists, does the deﬁnition recognize executive branch lobbyists?
source_quote: 1. In addition to legislative lobbyists, does the deﬁnition recognize executive branch lobbyists? No – 0 points Yes – 3 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:32)
notes: CPI explicitly carves out: "States did not receive points for including executive communication in the deﬁnition only when it directly related to legislative action." Implicit scope condition.

indicator_text: How much does an individual have to make/spend to qualify as a lobbyist or to prompt registration as a lobbyist, according to the deﬁnition?
scoring_rule: Qualiﬁcation threshold: More than $500 made/spent – 0 points; More than $100 made/spent – 1 point; More than $50 made/spent – 2 points; $50 or less made/spent – 3 points; Lobbyists qualify and must register no matter how much money made/spent – 4 points
source_quote: 2. How much does an individual have to make/spend to qualify as a lobbyist or to prompt registration as a lobbyist, according to the deﬁnition? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:44)
notes: 5-level ordinal threshold scale; 'no threshold' or 'any compensation triggers registration' = full points.

indicator_text: Is a lobbyist required to ﬁle a registration form?
source_quote: 3. Is a lobbyist required to ﬁle a registration form? No – 0 points Yes – 3 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:64)
notes: Refers to individual lobbyist registration, not employer/group.

indicator_text: How many days can lobbying take place before registration is required?
scoring_rule: 16 or more days – 0 points; 11 to 15 days – 1 point; 6 to 10 days – 2 points; 1 to 5 days – 3 points; 0 days – 4 points
source_quote: 4. How many days can lobbying take place before registration is required? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:68)
notes: 5-level ordinal grace-period scale.

indicator_text: Is subject matter or bill number to be addressed by a lobbyist required on registration forms?
scoring_rule: No bill number/subject matter required – 0 points; Subject matter only required – 1 point; Bill number required – 3 points
source_quote: 5. Is subject matter or bill number to be addressed by a lobbyist required on registration forms? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:75)
notes: 3-level categorical (none/subject only/bill number).

indicator_text: How often is registration by a lobbyist required?
scoring_rule: Once only – 0 points; Every two years – 1 point; Annually or more often – 2 points
source_quote: 6. How often is registration by a lobbyist required? Once only – 0 points Every two years – 1 point Annually or more often – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:84)
notes: Includes regular supplements/refilings.

indicator_text: Within how many days must a lobbyist notify the oversight agency of changes in registration?
scoring_rule: 16 – or more days – 0 points; 11 – 15 days – 1 point; 6 – 10 days – 2 points; 1 – 5 days – 3 points; 0 days – 4 points
source_quote: 7. Within how many days must a lobbyist notify the oversight agency of changes in registration? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:94)
notes: Specifically for changes in registration info; not regular spending reports.

indicator_text: Is a lobbyist required to submit a photograph with registration?
source_quote: 8. Is a lobbyist required to submit a photograph with registration? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:101)
notes: Pertains to identification badges.

indicator_text: Is a lobbyist required to identify by name each employer on the registration form?
source_quote: 9. Is a lobbyist required to identify by name each employer on the registration form? No – 0 points Yes – 1point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:108)
notes: Either single-form-multi-employer or per-employer-registration counts as full points.

indicator_text: Is a lobbyist required to include on the registration form any additional information about the type of lobbying work he or she does (i.e., compensated or non-compensated/contract or salaried)?
source_quote: 10. Is a lobbyist required to include on the registration form any additional information about the type of lobbying work he or she does (i.e., compensated or non-compensated/contract or salaried)? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:120)
notes: Full points if info appears on spending reports rather than registration; CPI relaxes the location.

indicator_text: Is a lobbyist required to ﬁle a spending report?
source_quote: 11. Is a lobbyist required to ﬁle a spending report? No – 0 points Yes – 3 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:131)
notes: Gateway item for the entire spending-disclosure category; CPI ties this to the statute's reporting section.

indicator_text: How often during each two-year cycle is a lobbyist required to report spending?
scoring_rule: 0 – 3 ﬁlings – 0 points; 4 – 6 ﬁlings – 1 point; 7 – 9 ﬁlings – 2 points; 10 – or more ﬁlings – 3 points
source_quote: 12. How often during each two-year cycle is a lobbyist required to report spending? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:136)
notes: Two-year cycle accommodates biennial legislatures; CPI gives a worked example for a 4-month-session state.

indicator_text: Is compensation/salary required to be reported by a lobbyist on spending reports?
source_quote: 13. Is compensation/salary required to be reported by a lobbyist on spending reports? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:151)
notes: Full points if information is on registration form instead.

indicator_text: Are summaries (totals) of spending classiﬁed by category types (i.e., gifts, entertainment, postage, etc.)?
source_quote: 14. Are summaries (totals) of spending classiﬁed by category types (i.e., gifts, entertainment, postage, etc.)? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:158)
notes: Single aggregate total without category breakdown = 0 points.

indicator_text: What spending must be itemized?
scoring_rule: No spending required to be itemized – 0 points; Itemization threshold: More than $100 – 1 point; Itemization threshold: More than $25 – 2 points; Itemization threshold: $25 and below – 3 points; All spending required to be itemized – 4 points
source_quote: 15. What spending must be itemized? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:165)
notes: 5-level ordinal threshold; 'itemized' = individual listing of single expenditures (CPI definition).

indicator_text: Is the lobbyist employer/principal on whose behalf the itemized expenditure was made required to be identiﬁed?
source_quote: 16. Is the lobbyist employer/principal on whose behalf the itemized expenditure was made required to be identiﬁed? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:180)
notes: Conditional on Q15: "If spending is not required to be itemized, a state received no points."

indicator_text: Is the recipient of the itemized expenditure required to be identiﬁed?
source_quote: 17. Is the recipient of the itemized expenditure required to be identiﬁed? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:189)
notes: Conditional on Q15: zero if itemization not required. Recipient = legislator/staff/family.

indicator_text: Is the date of the itemized expenditure required to be reported?
source_quote: 18. Is the date of the itemized expenditure required to be reported? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:196)
notes: Conditional on Q15: zero if itemization not required.

indicator_text: Is a description of the itemized expenditure required to be reported?
source_quote: 19. Is a description of the itemized expenditure required to be reported? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:202)
notes: Conditional on Q15: zero if itemization not required. Description or category (e.g., food/postage) qualifies.

indicator_text: Is subject matter or bill number to be addressed by a lobbyist required on spending reports?
scoring_rule: No bill number/subject matter required – 0 points; Subject matter only required – 1 point; Bill number required – 3 points
source_quote: 20. Is subject matter or bill number to be addressed by a lobbyist required on spending reports? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:213)
notes: Mirrors Q5 but on spending reports; same 3-level categorical.

indicator_text: Is spending on household members of public ofﬁcials by a lobbyist required to be reported?
source_quote: 21. Is spending on household members of public ofﬁcials by a lobbyist required to be reported? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:222)
notes: Implicit scope: depends on state's definition of household member.

indicator_text: Is a lobbyist required to disclose direct business associations with public ofﬁcials, candidates or members of their households?
source_quote: 22. Is a lobbyist required to disclose direct business associations with public ofﬁcials, candidates or members of their households? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:227)
notes: State-specific definitions of business association; implicit scope variation.

indicator_text: What is the statutory provision for a lobbyist giving and reporting gifts?
scoring_rule: Gifts are not reported – 0 points; Gifts are reported – 1 point; Gifts are limited and reported – 2 points; Gifts are prohibited – 3 points
source_quote: 23. What is the statutory provision for a lobbyist giving and reporting gifts? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:234)
notes: 4-level ordinal; gift prohibition with significant exceptions does NOT qualify for full points (CPI judgment call).

indicator_text: What is the statutory provision for a lobbyist giving and reporting campaign contributions?
scoring_rule: Campaign contributions allowed and not required to be disclosed on spending report/prohibited during session – 0 points; Campaign contributions allowed and not required to be disclosed on spending report/allowed during session – 0 points; Campaign contributions allowed and required to be disclosed on spending report/prohibited during session – 1 point; Campaign contributions allowed and required to be disclosed on spending report/allowed during session – 1point; Campaign contributions prohibited – 2 points
source_quote: 24. What is the statutory provision for a lobbyist giving and reporting campaign contributions? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:248)
notes: Composite: cross-tabs (allowed-vs-prohibited) x (disclosed-vs-not) x (session-vs-not). Five labeled levels but only 3 distinct point values (0/1/2). Session-timing distinction does not affect the point value.

indicator_text: Is a lobbyist who has done no spending during a ﬁling period required to make a report of no activity?
source_quote: 25. Is a lobbyist who has done no spending during a ﬁling period required to make a report of no activity? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:265)
notes: 'No activity' / null filing requirement.

indicator_text: Is an employer or principal of a lobbyist required to ﬁle a spending report?
source_quote: 26. Is an employer or principal of a lobbyist required to ﬁle a spending report? No – 0 point Yes – 3 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:275)
notes: Employer/principal/client of lobbyist.

indicator_text: Is compensation/salary required to be reported on employer/principal spending reports?
source_quote: 27. Is compensation/salary required to be reported on employer/principal spending reports? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:281)
notes: Mirrors Q13 from the employer side.

indicator_text: Does the oversight agency provide lobbyists/employers with online registration?
source_quote: 28. Does the oversight agency provide lobbyists/employers with online registration? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:287)
notes: CPI links this to ensuring electronic format and searchable database availability.

indicator_text: Does the oversight agency provide lobbyists/employers with online spending reporting?
source_quote: 29. Does the oversight agency provide lobbyists/employers with online spending reporting? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:295)
notes: Same rationale as Q28 applied to spending reports.

indicator_text: Does the oversight agency provide training about how to ﬁle registrations/spending reports electronically?
source_quote: 30. Does the oversight agency provide training about how to ﬁle registrations/spending reports electronically? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:307)
notes: 'Informational packet or any other conveyance of instruction' counts.

indicator_text: Location/format of registrations or active lobbyist directory
scoring_rule: Photocopies from ofﬁce only – 1 point; PDF or image ﬁles on the Web – 2 points; Searchable database on the Web – 3 points; Downloadable ﬁles/database – 4 points
source_quote: 31. Location/format of registrations or active lobbyist directory: Photocopies from ofﬁce only – 1 point PDF or image ﬁles on the Web – 2 points Searchable database on the Web – 3 points Downloadable ﬁles/database – 4 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:314)
notes: Highest-applies rule (CPI: choose item with highest point value that applies). 4-level ordinal access ladder. CPI defines 'searchable database' explicitly.

indicator_text: Location/format of spending reports
scoring_rule: Photocopies from ofﬁce only – 1 point; PDF or image ﬁles on the Web – 2 points; Searchable database on the Web – 3 points; Downloadable ﬁles/database – 4 points
source_quote: 32. Location/format of spending reports: Photocopies from ofﬁce only – 1 point PDF or image ﬁles on the Web – 2 points Searchable database on the Web – 3 points Downloadable ﬁles/database – 4 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:336)
notes: 'Highest-applies' rule. Mirrors Q31 for spending reports. CPI defines 'searchable database' interface (summary totals or full itemization listing).

indicator_text: Cost of copies
scoring_rule: 25 cents or more per page – 0 points; Less than 25 cents per page – 1 point
source_quote: 33. Cost of copies: 25 cents or more per page – 0 points Less than 25 cents per page – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:354)
notes: Tiered fee schedules: take rate appropriate for the first price quantity. Differential in/out-of-state pricing: use in-state price.

indicator_text: Are sample registration forms/spending reports available the Web?
source_quote: 34. Are sample registration forms/spending reports available the Web? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:369)
notes: Note typo in CPI source ('available the Web' missing 'on'). Captured verbatim.

indicator_text: Does the state agency provide an overall lobbying spending total by year?
source_quote: 35. Does the state agency provide an overall lobbying spending total by year? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:374)
notes: 2002 reference year for the data; provision via Web OR upon request both qualify.

indicator_text: Does the state agency provide an overall lobbying spending total by spending-report deadlines?
source_quote: 36. Does the state agency provide an overall lobbying spending total by spending-report deadlines? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:380)
notes: 2002 data; via Web or upon request.

indicator_text: Does the state agency provide an overall lobbying spending total by industries lobbyists represent?
source_quote: 37. Does the state agency provide an overall lobbying spending total by industries lobbyists represent? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:386)
notes: CPI flags: "Lobby spending is not commonly organized in this fashion." Web or upon request.

indicator_text: How often are lobby lists updated?
scoring_rule: Semi-annually or less often – 1 point; Monthly – 2 points; Weekly – 3 points; Daily – 4 points
source_quote: 38. How often are lobby lists updated? Semi-annually or less often – 1 point Monthly – 2 points Weekly – 3 points Daily – 4 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:398)
notes: 4-level ordinal frequency scale. No 0-point option visible.

indicator_text: Does the state have statutory auditing authority?
source_quote: 39. Does the state have statutory auditing authority? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:404)
notes: Authority only — does NOT measure use. Authority can rest with any state agency. Includes ability to request outside documents.

indicator_text: Does the state agency conduct mandatory reviews or audits?
source_quote: 40. Does the state agency conduct mandatory reviews or audits? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:413)
notes: Pairs with Q39 (action vs. authority). Random or universal reviews qualify; complaint-driven does not.

indicator_text: Is there a statutory penalty for late ﬁling of lobby registration form?
source_quote: 41. Is there a statutory penalty for late ﬁling of lobby registration form? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:426)
notes: Typically per-day fines.

indicator_text: Is there a statutory penalty for late ﬁling of a lobby spending report?
source_quote: 42. Is there a statutory penalty for late ﬁling of a lobby spending report? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:433)
notes: Typically per-day fines.

indicator_text: When was a penalty for late ﬁling of a lobby spending report last levied?
scoring_rule: More than 5 years – 0 points; 4 to 5 years – 1 point; 2 to 3 years – 2 points; 0 to 1 year – 3 points
source_quote: 43. When was a penalty for late ﬁling of a lobby spending report last levied? More than 5 years – 0 points 4 to 5 years – 1 point 2 to 3 years – 2 points 0 to 1 year – 3 points (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:440)
notes: Data source: state agency representative interview. Operational definition relies on official self-report — fallback source flag.

indicator_text: Is there a statutory penalty for incomplete ﬁling of a lobby registration form?
source_quote: 44. Is there a statutory penalty for incomplete ﬁling of a lobby registration form? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:447)
notes: Pairs Q41 (late) with Q44 (incomplete); 'incomplete' = missing pages/signatures/totals.

indicator_text: Is there a statutory penalty for incomplete ﬁling of a lobby spending report?
source_quote: 45. Is there a statutory penalty for incomplete ﬁling of a lobby spending report? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:460)
notes: Pairs Q42 (late) with Q45 (incomplete) for spending reports.

indicator_text: When was a penalty for incomplete ﬁling of a lobby spending report last levied?
scoring_rule: More than 5 years – 0 points; 4 – 5 years – 1 point; 2 – 3 years – 2 points; 0 – 1 year/agency does not accept incomplete ﬁlings – 3 points
source_quote: 46. When was a penalty for incomplete ﬁling of a lobby spending report last levied? (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:469)
notes: Data source: state agency representative interview. 'Agency does not accept incomplete filings' = 3 points (special bucket merged into top tier). Fallback source flag.

indicator_text: Does the state publish a list of delinquent ﬁlers either on the Web or in a printed document?
source_quote: 47. Does the state publish a list of delinquent ﬁlers either on the Web or in a printed document? No – 0 points Yes – 1 point (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:478)
notes: Web, newspaper, or other publication all qualify.

indicator_text: Is there a "cooling off" period required before legislators can register as lobbyists?
source_quote: 48. Is there a cooling off" period required before legislators can register as lobbyists? No – 0 points Yes – 2 points" (papers/text/CPI_2007__hired_guns_methodology.txt:490)
notes: Boolean only; CPI does not score the LENGTH of the cooling-off period. Scope: same-state legislators-to-lobbyists only.
